questions.question: What is 25% of £200?
questions.explanation: To find 25% of £200, we can calculate 200 ÷ 4 = £50 (because 25% is the same as 1/4). Or multiply: 200 × 0.25 = £50. ✓

questions.question: A shirt costs £40. In a sale, it is reduced by 20%. What is the sale price?
questions.explanation: First find 20% of £40: 40 ÷ 5 = £8 (because 20% = 1/5). Then subtract: £40 - £8 = £32. The sale price is £32. ✓

questions.question: In a class of 25 children, 15 are girls. What percentage are girls?
questions.explanation: To find the percentage: (15 ÷ 25) × 100 = 0.6 × 100 = 60%. So 60% of the class are girls. ✓

questions.question: A shop offers 10% off a coat costing £450. How much is the discount?
questions.explanation: To find 10% of any number, divide by 10. So £450 ÷ 10 = £45. ✓

questions.question: A bicycle was priced at £300. After a 15% discount, what is the new price?
questions.explanation: First find 15% of £300: 10% = £30, so 5% = £15, therefore 15% = £45. Then subtract: £300 - £45 = £255. ✓

questions.question: There are 86 pupils in Year 5. Half of them walk to school. How many is that? (50% of 86)
questions.explanation: 50% means half. To find half of 86, divide by 2: 86 ÷ 2 = 43. ✓

questions.question: A shop has 80 apples. 35% of them are green. How many green apples are there?
questions.explanation: Find 35% of 80: First find 10% = 8, so 30% = 24. Then 5% = 4. Add them: 24 + 4 = 28 green apples. ✓

questions.question: Which has the greatest value? 30% of 200, 25% of 240, or 20% of 300?
questions.explanation: Let's calculate each: 30% of 200 = 60; 25% of 240 = 60; 20% of 300 = 60. They are all equal to 60! ✓

questions.question: A football team won 18 out of 30 matches. What percentage did they win?
A swimming pool holds 160 litres. It is 75% full. How many litres of water are in the pool?
questions.explanation: 75% is the same as 3/4. To find 3/4 of 160: 160 ÷ 4 = 40, then 40 × 3 = 120. ✓

questions.question: A games console costs £240 after a 20% discount. What was the original price?
In a bag of 60 marbles, 15 are blue. What percentage are NOT blue?
questions.explanation: 15 out of 60 are blue, so that's 25%. The marbles that are NOT blue = 100% - 25% = 75%. ✓

questions.question: What is 5% of £840?
questions.explanation: To find 5%, first find 10% then divide by 2. 10% of £840 = £84, so 5% = £84 ÷ 2 = £42. ✓

questions.question: A book is reduced from £16 to £12. What is the percentage discount?
questions.explanation: The discount is £16 - £12 = £4. Now find what percentage £4 is of £16: (4 ÷ 16) × 100 = 25%. ✓

questions.question: Which is larger: 40% of 250 or 45% of 200?
A farmer has 120 sheep. 65% of them are female. How many male sheep does he have?
questions.explanation: If 65% are female, then 35% are male (100% - 65% = 35%). Find 35% of 120: 10% = 12, so 30% = 36, and 5% = 6. Therefore 35% = 36 + 6 = 42 male sheep. ✓

questions.question: In a spelling test, Emma got 0.35 of the questions right. What is her score as a percentage?
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100. So 0.35 × 100 = 35%. ✓

questions.question: A cinema has 400 seats. On Friday night, 85% of the seats were filled. How many seats were empty?
questions.explanation: If 85% were filled, then 15% were empty (100% - 85% = 15%). Find 15% of 400: 10% = 40, and 5% = 20, so 15% = 60 empty seats. ✓

questions.question: What is 12% of 350?
questions.explanation: Find 10% of 350 = 35. Then find 2% = 7 (because 2% is one-fifth of 10%). Add them: 35 + 7 = 42. ✓

questions.question: A jacket originally cost £75. After two successive discounts of 10% and then 10%, what is the final price?
questions.explanation: First discount: 10% of £75 = £7.50, so price = £67.50. Second discount: 10% of £67.50 = £6.75, so final price = £67.50 - £6.75 = £60.75. ✓ Remember: two 10% discounts don't equal 20%!

questions.question: In a test, Sarah scored 32 out of 40. What percentage did she score?
Which of these is the smallest? 0.15, 18%, 1/5, 0.2, 16%
questions.explanation: Convert all to decimals: 0.15 stays 0.15; 18% = 0.18; 1/5 = 0.2; 0.2 stays 0.2; 16% = 0.16. The smallest is 0.15. ✓

questions.question: A train ticket costs £45 for adults. Children pay 60% of the adult price. How much does a child ticket cost?
A shop increases all prices by 15%. A TV that cost £400 now costs how much?
What percentage of 1 metre is 35 centimetres?
questions.explanation: 1 metre = 100 cm. So 35 cm out of 100 cm = 35/100 = 35%. ✓

questions.question: In a survey of 200 people, 130 said 'yes'. What percentage said 'no'?
questions.explanation: If 130 said yes, then 70 said no (200 - 130 = 70). Calculate: (70 ÷ 200) × 100 = 35%. ✓

questions.question: A phone costs £180 after a 25% discount. What was the original price?
What is 45% of 220?
A school has 600 students. 48% are boys. How many are girls?
questions.explanation: If 48% are boys, then 52% are girls (100% - 48% = 52%). Find 52% of 600: 50% = 300, and 2% = 12, so 52% = 312 girls. ✓

questions.question: Which has the smallest value? 15% of 300, 18% of 200, or 12% of 400?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 15% of 300 = 45; 18% of 200 = 36; 12% of 400 = 48. The smallest is 18% of 200 = 36. ✓

questions.question: A recipe needs 250g of flour. Tom only wants to make 60% of the recipe. How much flour does he need?
A store sells 180 items in a week. 20% were sold on Saturday. How many items were sold on Saturday?
What percentage is 24 out of 96?
A library has 540 books. After buying more books, it has 15% more than before. How many books does it have now?
questions.explanation: Find 15% of 540: 10% = 54, and 5% = 27, so 15% = 81. Add to original: 540 + 81 = 621 books. ✓

questions.question: In a fruit bowl with 80 pieces of fruit, 35% are apples and 25% are oranges. How many are neither apples nor oranges?
questions.explanation: Apples and oranges together = 35% + 25% = 60%. So neither = 40% (100% - 60%). Find 40% of 80: 10% = 8, so 40% = 32 pieces of fruit. ✓

questions.question: A farm has 180 animals. 30% of them are sheep. How many sheep are there?
questions.explanation: To find 30%, first find 10% then multiply by 3. 10% of 180 = 18, so 30% = 18 × 3 = 54. ✓

questions.question: A backpack costs £80. During a clearance sale, it is reduced by 35%. What is the sale price?
In a box of 64 crayons, 16 are red. What percentage are red?
A school trip costs £240. A deposit of 15% is needed to book a place. How much is the deposit?
A tablet was priced at £68 after a 15% discount. What was the original price?
What is 60% of 350?
A garden has 150 plants. 45 are flowers. What percentage are flowers?
A school has 450 pupils. 72% attended the assembly. How many pupils were absent?
questions.explanation: If 72% attended, then 28% were absent (100% - 72%). Find 28% of 450: 10% = 45, so 20% = 90, and 8% = 36. Therefore 28% = 126. ✓

questions.question: What is 8% of £650?
A video game costs £90 after a 40% discount. What was the original price?
A baker uses 0.42 of a bag of flour for a cake. What percentage of the bag has been used?
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100. So 0.42 × 100 = 42%. ✓

questions.question: A school has 280 students in an assembly. 55% are female. How many are male?
questions.explanation: If 55% are female, then 45% are male (100% - 55%). Find 45% of 280: 10% = 28, so 40% = 112, and 5% = 14. Therefore 45% = 126. ✓

questions.question: A watch costs £120. If the price increases by 25%, what is the new price?
In a test, Noah scored 18 out of 45. What percentage did he score?
What is 35% of 420?
A camera costs £144 after a 20% discount. What was the original price?
A bottle contains 1.5 litres. If 540ml has been drunk, what percentage was drunk?
A gym membership costs £85. If it increases by 20%, what is the new price?
Which has the same value? 35% of 160, 40% of 140, or 32% of 175?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 35% of 160 = 56; 40% of 140 = 56; 32% of 175 = 56. They are all equal! ✓

questions.question: Ella scored 63 marks out of 90. What percentage did she score?
What is 12% of 550?
In a vote, 200 people participated. 65% voted 'yes'. How many voted 'no'?
questions.explanation: If 65% voted yes, then 35% voted no (100% - 65%). Find 35% of 200: 10% = 20, so 30% = 60, and 5% = 10. Therefore 35% = 70. ✓

questions.question: A bag contains 800g of rice. If 45% is used, how much remains?
questions.explanation: If 45% is used, then 55% remains (100% - 45%). Find 55% of 800g: 10% = 80g, so 50% = 400g, and 5% = 40g. Therefore 55% = 440g. ✓

questions.question: A pair of headphones costs £156 after a 20% discount. What was the original price?
What percentage is 48 out of 320?
A store offers two successive discounts of 15% and then 10% on an item originally priced at £200. What is the final price?
What is 18% of 350?
A sweet shop has 450 sweets. 38% are chocolate. How many are NOT chocolate?
questions.explanation: If 38% are chocolate, then 62% are not (100% - 38%). Find 62% of 450: 10% = 45, so 60% = 270, and 2% = 9. Therefore 62% = 279. ✓

questions.question: A coat costs £75. If the price increases by 32%, what is the new price?
Max scored 56 marks out of 140. What percentage did he score?
What is 22% of 650?
Ava has a 1.8 litre bottle of juice. She drinks 45%. How many millilitres remain?
questions.explanation: 1.8L = 1800ml. If 45% is drunk, then 55% remains. Find 55% of 1800ml: 10% = 180ml, so 50% = 900ml, and 5% = 90ml. Therefore 55% = 990ml. ✓

questions.question: A tablet costs £105 after a 30% discount. What was the original price?
Which of these has the smallest value? 28% of 200, 35% of 180, or 24% of 250?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 28% of 200 = 56; 35% of 180 = 63; 24% of 250 = 60. The smallest is 28% of 200 = 56. ✓

questions.question: What percentage is 27 out of 180?
A college has 750 students. 44% are studying science. How many are NOT studying science?
questions.explanation: If 44% study science, then 56% don't (100% - 44%). Find 56% of 750: 10% = 75, so 50% = 375, and 6% = 45. Therefore 56% = 420. ✓

questions.question: What is 16% of 875?
A keyboard costs £216 after a 10% discount. What was the original price?
What percentage is 35cm in 2 metres?
What is 85% of 360?
What percentage is 72 out of 240?
A phone bill is £140. If it increases by 18%, what is the new bill?
In an election, 500 people voted. 58% voted for candidate A. How many voted for other candidates?
questions.explanation: If 58% voted for A, then 42% voted for others (100% - 58%). Find 42% of 500: 10% = 50, so 40% = 200, and 2% = 10. Therefore 42% = 210. ✓

questions.question: What is 26% of 450?
A games console costs £168 after a 25% discount. What was the original price?
Lily uses 35% of a 600g bag of flour. How much flour did she use?
What percentage is 54 out of 180?
Which is largest? 42% of 250, 50% of 210, or 48% of 220?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 42% of 250 = 105; 50% of 210 = 105; 48% of 220 = 105.6. The third is largest. ✓

questions.question: A pair of trainers costs £95 after a 5% discount. What was the original price?
What is 14% of 725?
A school has 900 pupils. If 42% passed a swimming test, how many did not pass?
questions.explanation: If 42% passed, then 58% did not pass (100% - 42%). Find 58% of 900: 10% = 90, so 50% = 450, and 8% = 72. Therefore 58% = 522. ✓

questions.question: What percentage is 96 out of 160?
What is 38% of 550?
A dining table costs £135. If the price increases by 24%, what is the new price?
In a football match, a goalkeeper saved 0.68 of the shots on target. What is this as a percentage?
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100. So 0.68 × 100 = 68%. ✓

questions.question: A sofa costs £252 after a 10% discount. What was the original price?
A baker uses 28% of a 1.5kg bag of chocolate. How many grams did he use?
What percentage is 84 out of 210?
Which has the same value? 55% of 180, 60% of 165, or 45% of 220?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 55% of 180 = 99; 60% of 165 = 99; 45% of 220 = 99. They are all equal! ✓

questions.question: What is 19% of 680?
A television costs £192 after a 20% discount. What was the original price?
A school has 420 students. 35% are in Year 5. The rest are in Year 6. How many are in Year 6?
questions.explanation: If 35% are Year 5, then 65% are Year 6 (100% - 35%). Find 65% of 420: 10% = 42, so 60% = 252, and 5% = 21. Therefore 65% = 273. ✓

questions.question: What is 32% of 875?
What percentage is 108 out of 360?
A haircut costs £88. If the price increases by 15%, what is the new price?
A juice carton contains 2.5 litres. If 750ml is poured out, what percentage has been poured out?
Which of these has the smallest value? 36% of 250, 40% of 250, or 48% of 200?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 36% of 250 = 90; 40% of 250 = 100; 48% of 200 = 96. The smallest is 36% of 250 = 90. ✓

questions.question: A bicycle costs £117 after a 35% discount. What was the original price?
What is 27% of 740?
In a survey, 650 people responded. 58% agreed. How many disagreed?
questions.explanation: If 58% agreed, then 42% disagreed (100% - 58%). Find 42% of 650: 10% = 65, so 40% = 260, and 2% = 13. Therefore 42% = 273. ✓

questions.question: What percentage is 135 out of 450?
A restaurant bill is £210. If it increases by 16%, what is the new total?
What is 46% of 950?
A monitor costs £234 after a 10% discount. What was the original price?
A zoo has 800 animals. 62% are mammals. How many are NOT mammals?
questions.explanation: If 62% are mammals, then 38% are not (100% - 62%). Find 38% of 800: 10% = 80, so 30% = 240, and 8% = 64. Therefore 38% = 304. ✓

questions.question: A jacket originally costs £300. After two successive discounts of 20% and then 15%, what is the final price?
What percentage is 144 out of 480?
What is 34% of 850?
A car costs £161 after a 30% discount. What was the original price?
A swimming pool is 0.58 full of water. What is this as a percentage?
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100. So 0.58 × 100 = 58%. ✓

questions.question: Which is largest? 52% of 250, 65% of 200, or 48% of 275?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 52% of 250 = 130; 65% of 200 = 130; 48% of 275 = 132. The third is largest. ✓

questions.question: A concert ticket costs £104. If the price increases by 22%, what is the new price?
What is 24% of 625?
A park has 560 trees. 45% are oak. How many trees are NOT oak?
questions.explanation: If 45% are oak, then 55% are not (100% - 45%). Find 55% of 560: 10% = 56, so 50% = 280, and 5% = 28. Therefore 55% = 308. ✓

questions.question: What percentage is 168 out of 560?
A printer costs £189 after a 30% discount. What was the original price?
What is 42% of 725?
What percentage is 250cm in 4 metres?
A membership fee is £95. If the price increases by 28%, what is the new fee?
What percentage is 192 out of 640?
A painting costs £133 after a 5% discount. What was the original price?
What is 36% of 825?
A library has 900 books. 48% are fiction. How many are non-fiction?
questions.explanation: If 48% are fiction, then 52% are non-fiction (100% - 48%). Find 52% of 900: 10% = 90, so 50% = 450, and 2% = 18. Therefore 52% = 468. ✓

questions.question: Which of these has the smallest value? 44% of 300, 55% of 260, or 52% of 260?
questions.explanation: Calculate each: 44% of 300 = 132; 55% of 260 = 143; 52% of 260 = 135.2. The smallest is 44% of 300 = 132. ✓

questions.question: A desk costs £198 after a 12% discount. What was the original price?
What is 28% of 675?
What percentage is 216 out of 720?
A holiday costs £155. If the price increases by 26%, what is the new price?
In a class reading challenge, Olivia completed 0.74 of the books on the list. What is this as a percentage?
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100. So 0.74 × 100 = 74%. ✓

questions.question: What is 54% of 950?
A coat costs £60. In a sale, it is reduced by 10%. What is the sale price?
A skateboard costs £45. It is reduced by 20% in a sale. What is the sale price?
A laptop costs £480. In a clearance sale, it is reduced by 25%. What is the sale price?
A pair of jeans costs £55. They are reduced by 30% in a sale. What is the sale price?
A scooter costs £125. The price is reduced by 12% in a sale. What is the sale price?
A school bag costs £36. In a sale, it is reduced by 15%. What is the sale price?
A board game costs £28. It is reduced by 25% in a sale. What is the sale price?
A dress costs £64. In the January sales, it is reduced by 35%. What is the sale price?
A television costs £750. It is first reduced by 20%, then a further 5% off the reduced price. What is the final price?
questions.explanation: First reduction: 20% of £750 = £150, so price = £600. Second reduction: 5% of £600 = £30, so final price = £600 - £30 = £570. ✓

questions.question: A toy car costs £18. It is reduced by 50% in a half-price sale. What is the sale price?
A football shirt costs £42. It is reduced by 15% in a sale. How much do you save?
A shop reduces all prices by 18%. A jacket originally costs £85. What is the sale price?
A book costs £12. It is reduced by 25% in a sale. What is the sale price?
A pair of boots costs £90. They are reduced by 22% in a sale. What is the sale price?
A tablet costs £320. It is reduced by 35% in a closing-down sale. What is the sale price?
A coat costs £74. It is reduced by 40% in a sale. What is the sale price?
A bicycle costs £260. In a summer sale, it is reduced by 15%. What is the sale price?
A guitar costs £180. It is on sale with 28% off. What is the sale price?
A train ticket costs £54. If the price increases by 10%, what is the new price?
A bus fare costs £2.40. If it increases by 25%, what is the new fare?
A cinema ticket costs £9.50. If the price increases by 20%, what is the new price?
A school dinner costs £2.80. If the price increases by 15%, what is the new price?
A gym membership costs £45 per month. If it increases by 12%, what is the new monthly cost?
A magazine costs £4.00. If the price increases by 50%, what is the new price?
A car insurance premium is £360. If it increases by 8%, what is the new premium?
A monthly phone contract costs £32. If it increases by 14%, what is the new cost?
Which is larger: 0.45 or 40%?
questions.explanation: Convert to the same form: 0.45 = 45%, and 40% = 0.40. Since 45% > 40%, 0.45 is larger. ✓

questions.question: Put these in order from smallest to largest: 3/8, 35%, 0.4
Which of these is equal to 1/2? 45%, 50%, 55%, 60%, 65%
Which is the odd one out? 0.75, 3/4, 70%, 75%
questions.explanation: 0.75 = 75%, 3/4 = 75%, 75% = 75%. But 70% is different. So 70% is the odd one out. ✓

questions.question: Put these in order from largest to smallest: 7/20, 0.38, 36%
questions.explanation: Convert all to decimals: 7/20 = 0.35, 0.38 stays as 0.38, 36% = 0.36. Largest to smallest: 0.38 > 0.36 > 0.35, so the order is 0.38, 36%, 7/20. ✓

questions.question: In a science experiment, a plant grew 0.15 metres. What is this as a percentage of 1 metre?
questions.explanation: 0.15 metres out of 1 metre = 0.15. To convert to a percentage, multiply by 100: 0.15 × 100 = 15%. ✓

questions.question: A jar is 0.85 full of jam. What percentage of the jar is empty?
Convert 0.06 to a percentage.
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100. So 0.06 × 100 = 6%. ✓

questions.question: A battery is at 0.72 charge. What percentage of charge remains?
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100. So 0.72 × 100 = 72%. ✓

questions.question: A shop increases the price of a £60 item by 10%, then increases the new price by another 10%. What is the final price?
questions.explanation: First increase: 10% of £60 = £6, so price = £66. Second increase: 10% of £66 = £6.60, so final price = £66 + £6.60 = £72.60. Note: two 10% increases do NOT equal 20%! ✓

questions.question: A coat costs £200. It is first increased by 25%, then the new price is reduced by 20%. What is the final price?
questions.explanation: First: 25% of £200 = £50, so price = £250. Then: 20% of £250 = £50, so final price = £250 - £50 = £200. Back to where it started! ✓

questions.question: A price increases by 10% and then decreases by 10%. Is the final price higher, lower, or the same as the original?
questions.explanation: Try £100: increase 10% → £110. Then decrease 10% of £110 = £11, so £110 - £11 = £99. The final price is LOWER because the 10% decrease is applied to a larger amount. ✓

questions.question: A bicycle costs £180. It is reduced by 15%, then the sale price is reduced by a further 10%. What is the final price?
questions.explanation: First reduction: 15% of £180 = £27, so price = £153. Second reduction: 10% of £153 = £15.30, so final price = £153 - £15.30 = £137.70. ✓

questions.question: A school has 400 pupils. 40% are boys. 50% of the girls play netball. How many girls play netball?
questions.explanation: 60% are girls (100% - 40%). Girls = 60% of 400 = 240. Girls playing netball = 50% of 240 = 120. ✓

questions.question: A baker makes 240 cakes. 30% are chocolate and the rest are vanilla. He sells 75% of the vanilla cakes. How many vanilla cakes does he sell?
questions.explanation: Vanilla cakes = 70% of 240 = 168. Sold = 75% of 168: 168 × 3/4 = 126 vanilla cakes sold. ✓

questions.question: In a school of 500 pupils, 52% are girls. 25% of the girls and 30% of the boys play a musical instrument. How many pupils play a musical instrument?
questions.explanation: Girls = 52% of 500 = 260. Boys = 240. Girls playing = 25% of 260 = 65. Boys playing = 30% of 240 = 72. Total = 65 + 72 = 137. ✓
